script:
It is early.

You said you'd ship the OATH v1 spec to GitHub and send Oliver the link before noon.

That was last night. That was a real commitment.

Kobe Bryant was in the gym at four in the morning. No crowd. No camera. Four hundred makes before most people set an alarm. Not because he felt like it. Because he said he would.

You said the same thing.

trust the work

Open the spec doc. Write the wedge section. That is the first five minutes. That is the only thing that exists right now.

Forty-five minutes. Phone face down.

Oliver gets that link before noon. The spec ships today. Not because conditions are right. Because you committed.

Write.

attempts.raw_response:
{
  "script": "Alarm's off.

You said: ship the OATH v1 spec to GitHub and send Oliver the link before noon.

Not someday. Today. Before noon.

Kobe was in the gym at 4am. No crowd. No cameras. Just reps. Same work, every morning, before the world woke up.

You do the same.

Trust the work.

Open the spec doc. Write the wedge section. That's the first five minutes. Nothing else exists.

Forty-five minutes. Phone face down.

Oliver gets that link.

Write.",
  "estimated_duration_seconds": 58,
  "word_count": 84
}

variant: more_clipped

user's hero (reference by concrete habit, never by invented quote): Kobe Bryant
user's grounding phrase (use verbatim, do not paraphrase): "trust the work"

Target read duration: 60 seconds (~150 words at 150 wpm).

Output as JSON:

  "estimated_duration_seconds": <integer>,

}

Output the JSON only. No preamble. No explanation. No markdown fence.
